'''''UFC 236: Holloway vs. Poirier 2'''''  was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] held on April 13, 2019 at the [[State Farm Arena]] in [[Atlanta, Georgia]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmaweekly.com/ufc-releases-2019-second-quarter-schedule|title=UFC releases 2019 second quarter schedule|date=2018-12-30|publisher=mmaweekly.com|author=Staff|access-date=2018-12-31}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/26058362/max-holloway-dustin-poirier-headline-ufc-236%3fplatform=amp|title=Max Holloway-Dustin Poirier to headline UFC 236 in Atlanta|date=2019-02-22|work=espn.com|author=Brett Okamoto|access-date=2019-02-22}}</ref>

==Background==
In the United States, per an extension of its new rights agreements with [[ESPN]] through 2025, [[ESPN+]] became the exclusive distributor of UFC pay-per-views for residential customers beginning at UFC 236: viewers must have an active ESPN+ subscription in order to purchase and stream PPV events, and they can no longer be purchased through other outlets (such as television providers). At the same time, the price of the PPV was lowered from $64.99 to $59.99, and a promotional offer was launched allowing new subscribers to purchase a bundle of the PPV and a one-year subscription to ESPN+ for $20 more.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportsbusinessdaily.com:443/en/Daily/Closing%20Bell/2019/03/18/ESPN%20UFC.aspx|title=ESPN extends with UFC; ESPN+ becomes exclusive PPV provider|publisher=sportsbusinessdaily.com|author=John Ourand|date=2019-03-18|access-date=2019-03-18}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://variety.com/2019/digital/news/ufc-ppv-espn-plus-pay-per-view-1203165507/|title=All UFC Pay-Per-View events moving to ESPN+ exclusively in U.S. through 2025|publisher=variety.com|author=Todd Spangler|date=2019-03-18|access-date=2019-03-18}}</ref>

The event was headlined by an interim [[UFC Lightweight Championship]] bout between the then reigning [[UFC Featherweight Championship|UFC Featherweight Champion]] [[Max Holloway]] and [[Dustin Poirier]].<ref name="main&comain">{{Cite web|url=https://www.bjpenn.com/mma-news/ufc/max-holloway-returns-at-ufc-236-for-interim-lightweight-title-fight-with-dustin-poirier/|title=Max Holloway returns at UFC 236 for interim lightweight title fight with Dustin Poirier|publisher=bjpenn.com|author=Cole Shelton|date=2019-02-23|access-date=2019-02-23}}</ref> The two previously met in a featherweight bout at [[UFC 143]] where Poirier won by submission in the first round.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmajunkie.com/2012/02/ufc-143-results-max-holloway-vs-dustin-poirier/amp|title=UFC 143 results: Dustin Poirier taps Max Holloway with dual-submission finish|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2012-02-05|access-date=2012-02-05}}</ref>

An interim [[UFC Middleweight Championship]] bout between [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Jones vs. Team Sonnen]] middleweight winner [[Kelvin Gastelum]] and former [[Glory (kickboxing)|Glory Kickboxing]] middleweight champion [[Israel Adesanya]] served as the co-main event.<ref name="interim185">{{Cite web|url=http://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/26032455/israel-adesanya-named-replacement-opponent-kelvin-gastelum-ufc-236|title=Israel Adesanya named as replacement opponent for Kelvin Gastelum at UFC 236|author=Brett Okamoto|work=espn.com|date=2018-02-19|access-date=2019-02-19}}</ref>

A bantamweight bout between Boston Salmon and [[Khalid Taha]] was initially scheduled for [[The Ultimate Fighter: Heavy Hitters#The Ultimate Fighter 28 Finale|The Ultimate Fighter: Heavy Hitters Finale]]. However, the pairing was removed from the card for undisclosed reasons and instead took place at this event.<ref name="boston">{{Cite web|url=http://mmadna.nl/boston-salmon-vs-khalid-taha-opnieuw-ingepland-ditmaal-voor-ufc-236/|title=Boston Salmon vs. Khalid rescheduled, this time for UFC 236|publisher=mmadna.nl|author=Marcel Dorff|date=2019-02-06|access-date=2019-02-06|language=nl}}</ref>

[[Paige VanZant]] was briefly linked to a match-up with [[Poliana Botelho]] at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://sportv.globo.com/site/combate/noticia/poliana-botelho-enfrenta-paige-vanzant-no-ufc-226-dia-13-de-abril.ghtml|title=Poliana Botelho faces Paige VanZant at UFC 236, April 13|publisher=sportv.globo.com|author=Raphael Marinho|date=2020-02-20|access-date=2019-02-20|language=pt}}</ref> However, just days after the pairing was leaked, VanZant announced that she would not be competing on the card due to a fractured right arm.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.com/2019/02/ufc-paige-vanzant-injured-broken-arm-fracture-surgery|title=UFC's Paige VanZant says she's suffered another arm fracture|author=Mike Bohn|publisher=mmajunkie.com|date=2018-02-28|access-date=2019-03-01}}</ref> VanZant was replaced by [[Lauren Mueller]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmadna.nl/paige-vanzant-geblesseerd-poliana-botelho-treft-nu-lauren-mueller-tijdens-ufc-236/|title=Paige VanZant geblesseerd, Poliana Botelho treft nu Lauren Mueller tijdens UFC 236|author=Marcel Dorff|date=28 February 2019 |access-date=2019-03-01|language=nl}}</ref>

== Bonus awards ==
The following fighters were awarded $50,000 bonuses:<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bloodyelbow.com/2019/4/14/18309887/ufc-236-post-fight-bonuses-adesanya-gastelum-and-poirier-holloway-both-net-fotn-honors|title=UFC 236 post-fight bonuses: Adesanya-Gastelum and Poirier-Holloway both net FOTN honors|publisher=bloodyelbow.com|author=Stephie Haynes|date=2019-04-14|access-date=2019-04-14|archive-date=2019-04-14|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190414063712/https://www.bloodyelbow.com/2019/4/14/18309887/ufc-236-post-fight-bonuses-adesanya-gastelum-and-poirier-holloway-both-net-fotn-honors|url-status=dead}}</ref>
* '''Fight of the Night: Dustin Poirier vs. Max Holloway''' and '''Kelvin Gastelum vs. Israel Adesanya'''
*'''Performance of the Night:''' No bonus awarded.

==Reported payout==
The following is the reported payout to the fighters as reported to the Georgia Athletic and Entertainment Commission. It does not include sponsor money and also does not include the UFC's traditional "fight night" bonuses. The total disclosed payroll for the event was $1,972,000.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.com/2019/04/ufc-236-salaries-max-holloway-israel-adesanya-lead-payouts|title=UFC 236 salaries: Max Holloway, Israel Adesanya lead payouts with $350,000|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2019-04-15|access-date=2019-04-15}}</ref>

*'''Dustin Poirier:''' $250,000 (no win bonus) def. Max Holloway: $350,000
*'''Israel Adesanya:''' $350,000 (no win bonus) def. Kelvin Gastelum: $150,000
*'''Khalil Rountree Jr.:''' $70,000 (includes $35,000 win bonus) def. Eryk Anders: $50,000
*'''Dwight Grant:''' $24,000 (includes $12,000 win bonus) def. Alan Jouban: $43,000
*'''Nikita Krylov:''' $160,000 (includes $80,000 win bonus) def. Ovince Saint Preux: $86,000
*'''Matt Frevola:''' $20,000 (includes $10,000 win bonus) def. Jalin Turner: $12,000
*'''Alexandre Pantoja:''' $36,000 (includes $18,000 win bonus) def. Wilson Reis: $34,000
*'''Max Griffin:''' $40,000 (includes $20,000 win bonus) def. Zelim Imadaev: $10,000
*'''Khalid Taha:''' $20,000 (includes $10,000 win bonus) def. Boston Salmon: $10,000
*'''Belal Muhammad:''' $70,000 (includes $35,000 win bonus) def.  Curtis Millender: $31,000
*'''Montel Jackson:''' $24,000 (includes $12,000 win bonus) def. Andre Soukhamthath: $22,000
*'''Poliana Botelho:''' $50,000 (includes $25,000 win bonus) def. Lauren Mueller: $12,000
*'''Brandon Davis:''' $36,000 (includes $18,000 win bonus) def. Randy Costa: $12,000
